spec_match.rationale: Exercises ::first-letter, ::first-line, ::selection, :hover::before, :hover::after, ::before, ::after — broad coverage of the newly-unblocked pseudo set, with each theme reusing different chains.

interactive.rationale: Four theme buttons swap one shadow-DOM component live; selector log updates per theme with pre-130 pass/fail annotation; visitor can also hover the badge and select body text to see ::after / ::selection respond.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb promises four themes built from chains like ::part(title)::first-letter and ::part(badge):hover::after with pre-130 rejection reporting — the page implements both.

edge_cases.rationale: The selector log labels each chain as 'rejected pre-130' or 'OK pre-130' but the rejection heuristic in JS is approximate (regex match) rather than a real CSS.supports() probe. A genuine probe per selector would be more honest. Also no theme exercises a selector that should still fail in Chrome 130.

references.rationale: See-also section links to the feature index and the spec but NOT to https://chromestatus.com/feature/5195333643272192 directly. Rule #3 requires the chromestatus link on every page.

design_system.rationale: All themes draw from CSS variable palette including the terminal theme which uses --text-black/--accent-emerald contrast (passes AA easily); theme-btn override sets both background and color together.

openQuestions.title: Add the chromestatus link to references
openQuestions.detail: The 'see also' section omits the direct https://chromestatus.com/feature/5195333643272192 link. CLAUDE.md rule #3 requires every page to carry it as a self-heal anchor in case folder slugs ever drift. Add it.

openQuestions.title: Replace the regex-based pre-130 heuristic with real CSS.supports() probes
openQuestions.detail: preChrome130Accepts() approximates the old grammar with a regex; this is fine for show but the page already runs in Chrome 130+ where the new chains succeed. Hard-coding a static expectation table per selector or comparing against a known reference list of pre-130-allowed pseudos would be more accurate than parsing strings in JS.

openQuestions.title: Add a fifth theme that exercises a still-rejected selector
openQuestions.detail: All four themes use only newly-OK chains. A fifth 'experimental' theme that intentionally includes a combinator (::part(card) > .x) or :has() would show what the spec still forbids and reinforce the boundary.

summary: Strong showcase of how the new chains compose into real design-system themes. The missing chromestatus link is the only real issue; the rest is opportunity for depth rather than correctness.
